_note: THE CARD DUEL — a pure-data probe of TURN-BASED play + deep symbolic identity, the two untested continents. Two duelists at a table: draw named cards (rank = the symbol), and on YOUR turn play your BEST card — damage = its rank minus half the foe's guard (target-context armor), that SPECIFIC card is discarded (pick-marks it, it despawns itself), and the turn passes to the foe (an affect writes the table's turn stat). First to fell the other stands victorious. STRESS POINTS: (1) can data enforce strict alternation when the engine applies everything sequentially within a tick? (2) play-ONE-specific-card = edge_max to read it + @max pick to mark it + self-despawn to discard it; (3) hand limits via typed claim; (4) deck exhaustion + a dealer restocking on a clock; (5) a deterministic victor.

actions.name: face_your_opponent
actions._note: wire the duel: an edge to the table (to pass the turn through) and an edge to the other duelist (to strike through). link picks a random co-located peer of the kind — with one table and one other duelist, it lands immediately.

actions.name: play_your_best_card
actions._note: the symbolic heart: read the held card's rank (edge_max), strike the foe by it minus half their guard (target-context), mark THAT card played (@max pick), pass the turn (affect the table). The card despawns itself — discard as narration.
actions._turn_note: TWO turn regimes, both pure data. (a) turn-STAT passing (eq(edge_max(at,turn),side)): strict alternation, but both may answer within ONE tick — sub-tick, invisible to events (the tick is the quantum of observation). (b) tick-PARITY (below): one play per tick, every turn-pass observable/narratable. Pick per world.

events.label: → the turn passes to the SECOND chair
← the turn passes to the FIRST chair
a card flashes onto the table and is spent
the dealer fans out a fresh card
slumps from the chair — the duel is lost
★ stands victorious in the lantern light
